items.title: 习近平：前瞻布局和发展未来产业
items.summary: 中国政府网要闻列表，覆盖国务院、部门政策和公共事务即时发布
items.contentExcerpt: 今天进行二十届中央政治局第二十四次集体学习，内容是前瞻布局和发展未来产业，主要是总结近年来我国未来产业发展情况，分析世界未来产业发展趋势，对培育发展未来产业进行思考。

items.title: 习近平给中共一大纪念馆、南湖革命纪念馆少先队红领巾讲解员的回信
items.summary: 中国政府网要闻列表，覆盖国务院、部门政策和公共事务即时发布
items.contentExcerpt: 来信收到了。得知你们在一大会址和南湖红船旁，用心用情讲述党的历史、革命故事和英雄事迹，厚植了爱党、爱国、爱社会主义的情感，得到了锻炼与成长，我感到欣慰。

items.contentExcerpt: 在“六一”国际儿童节即将到来之际，中共中央总书记、国家主席、中央军委主席习近平给中共一大纪念馆、南湖革命纪念馆少先队红领巾讲解员回信，对他们予以亲切勉励，并祝他们和全国的少年儿童节日快乐。

items.title: 努力成为跨越太平洋的“友谊使者”——习近平主席复信激励中美青少年和友好人士为两国关系稳定健康可持续发展贡献力量
items.summary: 中国政府网要闻列表，覆盖国务院、部门政策和公共事务即时发布
items.contentExcerpt: “很高兴得知两国学生搭乘‘中美青年友谊号’，共同经历了难忘的友谊之旅。”5月23日，国家主席习近平复信参加“共航蔚蓝：中美青年友谊行”活动的两国学生，对两国青少年的友好交流给予充分肯定，并寄予殷切期望。

items.title: Hubble Spies Faint Irregular Galaxy
items.summary: This NASA Hubble Space Telescope image released on May 27, 2026, features the dwarf irregular galaxy ESO 490-017, roughly 12,000 light-years in diameter and some 23 million light-years away in the constellation Canis Major. The galaxy’s low surface brightness makes it appear as a faint, starry swarm behind brighter foreground stars that are easily recognized by their diffraction spikes. […]
items.contentExcerpt: This NASA Hubble Space Telescope image captures the faint glow of the dwarf irregular galaxy ESO 490-017. Credit: NASA, ESA, R. Tully (University of Hawaii); Image Processing: G. items.title: Gaza children trapped ‘in an endless cycle of suffering’: UNICEF
items.summary: The dire conditions in the Gaza Strip are trapping children “in an endless cycle of suffering” and their heartbroken parents can only look on, an official with the UN child rights agency UNICEF said on Friday in a fresh appeal for greater humanitarian access to support families in the war-ravaged enclave.
items.contentExcerpt: The dire conditions in the Gaza Strip are trapping children “in an endless cycle of suffering” and their heartbroken parents can only look on, an official with the UN child rights agency UNICEF said on Friday in a fresh appeal for greater humanitarian access to support families in the war-ravaged enclave.

items.title: Borrowed boots, borrowed joy: Football thrives in Gaza camps
items.summary: Matches organised by a former professional player are providing a brief respite from the harsh reality of life for the thousands living in overcrowded tents, schools or damaged buildings in the shattered Occupied Palestinian Territory of Gaza.
items.contentExcerpt: Matches organised by a former professional player are providing a brief respite from the harsh reality of life for the thousands living in overcrowded tents, schools or damaged buildings in the shattered Occupied Palestinian Territory of Gaza.

items.title: NASA Awards Contract for Johnson Space Center Infrastructure
items.summary: NASA has selected seven companies to provide construction, revitalization, and infrastructure improvements at the agency’s Johnson Space Center in Houston. The Johnson Space Center Multiple Award Construction Contract supports up to $300 million in upgrades to mission‑support facilities, utilities, and equipment across the NASA Johnson campus. All funds must be obligated by Sept. 30, 2026. […]
items.contentExcerpt: NASA has selected seven companies to provide construction, revitalization, and infrastructure improvements at the agency’s Johnson Space Center in Houston. The Johnson Space Center Multiple Award Construction Contract supports up to $300 million in upgrades to mission‑support facilities, utilities, and equipment across the NASA Johnson campus. All funds must be obligated by Sept. 30, 2026. The indefinite-delivery/indefinite-quantity award enables rapid execution of facility projects essential...

items.title: NASA Hosts SpaceX Crew-11 Astronauts for Public Event at Headquarters
items.summary: NASA will host a public event featuring three crew members from the agency’s SpaceX Crew-11 mission at 11 a.m. EDT Monday, June 1. The event, which takes place during the crew’s standard postflight visit, will be held in the Webb Auditorium at NASA Headquarters in the Mary W. Jackson building, 300 E. Street SW in […]
items.contentExcerpt: NASA’s SpaceX Crew-11 astronauts gather together for a crew portrait wearing their Dragon pressure suits during a suit verification check inside the International Space Station’s Kibo laboratory module. Clockwise from bottom left are, NASA astronaut Mike Fincke, Roscosmos cosmonaut Oleg Platonov, NASA astronaut Zena Cardman, and JAXA (Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency) astronaut Kimiya Yui. Credit: NASA NASA will host a public event featuring three crew members from the agency’s...

items.title: NASA’s Roman Space Telescope Primary Mirror Gets Last Look
items.summary: Engineers at NASA’s Goddard Space Flight Center in Greenbelt, Maryland, have completed their final inspection of a key element for the agency’s Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope: the primary mirror. This 7.9-foot (2.4-meter) mirror will collect and focus light from cosmic objects near and far, helping Roman capture stunning panoramas of space. “The Roman engineering […]
items.contentExcerpt: 4 Min Read NASA’s Roman Space Telescope Primary Mirror Gets Last Look This photo peers down the barrel of the Roman telescope with its visor-like sunshade deployed. Credits: NASA/Sydney Rohde Engineers at NASA’s Goddard Space Flight Center in Greenbelt, Maryland, have completed their final inspection of a key element for the agency’s Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope: the primary mirror. This 7.9-foot (2.4-meter) mirror will collect and focus light from cosmic objects near and far, helping Ro...

items.title: Gaza: 26 killed over Eid holiday, UN rights investigators report
items.summary: At least 26 Palestinians were killed in the Gaza Strip since Tuesday – the eve of one of the most important holidays in Islam – the UN human rights office, OHCHR, reported on Friday.
items.contentExcerpt: At least 26 Palestinians were killed in the Gaza Strip since Tuesday – the eve of one of the most important holidays in Islam – the UN human rights office, OHCHR, reported on Friday.

items.title: World News in Brief: Drone strike in Romania condemned, Lebanon and South Sudan updates

items.title: Banning children from social media is not enough, UN warns – platforms must be made safe by design
items.summary: Blocking children from social media is no substitute for making platforms safe in the first place, the UN human rights office warned Friday, as it issued a 10-point framework urging governments and tech companies to go further and faster to protect children online.
items.contentExcerpt: Blocking children from social media is no substitute for making platforms safe in the first place, the UN human rights office warned Friday, as it issued a 10-point framework urging governments and tech companies to go further and faster to protect children online.

items.title: Conflict-related sexual violence cases more than doubled in 2025, UN warns
items.summary: Nearly 10,000 cases of conflict-related sexual violence were recorded worldwide last year – more than double the previous year's figure – as rape, sexual slavery and abduction were deployed as weapons of war across Africa, the Middle East, Europe and the Caribbean.
items.contentExcerpt: Nearly 10,000 cases of conflict-related sexual violence were recorded worldwide last year – more than double the previous year's figure – as rape, sexual slavery and abduction were deployed as weapons of war across Africa, the Middle East, Europe and the Caribbean.

items.title: ‘A disease you get when you care for someone’: WHO on the Ebola frontline
items.summary: Two weeks into the latest deadly Ebola outbreak, the World Health Organization (WHO) estimates there are now 906 suspected cases of Ebola in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), including 223 suspected deaths.
items.contentExcerpt: Two weeks into the latest deadly Ebola outbreak, the World Health Organization (WHO) estimates there are now 906 suspected cases of Ebola in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), including 223 suspected deaths.

items.title: UN honours blue helmets and calls for renewed investment in peace
items.summary: Peacekeepers are serving in a dangerous era of mounting global tensions and tight resources. UN chief António Guterres is now calling for greater political backing and reliable financial support for ‘blue helmets’ who put themselves in harm’s way.
items.contentExcerpt: Peacekeepers are serving in a dangerous era of mounting global tensions and tight resources. UN chief António Guterres is now calling for greater political backing and reliable financial support for ‘blue helmets’ who put themselves in harm’s way.
